flags.rewound_from_stage0_5_pivot: Case 6b (spirit) + regression pattern (a): boundary stage_0.5_to_0 with same flag — Conjecture-2 witness lacks finite LP certificate and load-bearing pastability lemma is asserted not derived — repeating across all 3 attempts; proposal_promise_gap is kernel_substituted/direction_only/constructive_object_missing; reviewers explicitly point to a recast (factual-compatibility theorem from the validated Conjecture-1 counterexample) rather than a fixable Stage-0 derivation step. The currently solid material (Conjecture-1 refutation + outer-envelope inclusion) does not clear the flagship novelty floor on its own, and the witness side cannot be made verifiable without research-grade scaffolding the proposal didn't supply. Pivot the angle.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of distributional treatment effects via optimal-transport extremal couplings under marginal-overlap restrictions on counterfactual outcome distributions
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-repo prior art is the failed eid_dist_synth_control proposal/reviews, which requested an LP/Frechet-Makarov correction for marginal DTE reporting, plus the exhausted pid_ot_dte_marginal angles 0--3 on path envelopes, finite diagnostics, dependence lattices, and lifted-OT pasting. The new gap is covariate aggregation: an iff frontier for when the conditional Minkowski DTE path set equals the pooled-marginal OT path set, with A_D(P) as the focal scalar.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are FanGuerreZhu2017 on covariate tightening, FirpoRidder2019 on finite-vector/functionals beyond pointwise Makarov bounds, and FanPark2010 on sharp DTE bounds from marginals. The proposal differs by asking for a necessary-and-sufficient support-function diagnostic for pooled versus conditional covariate aggregation, plus a computable epsilon_D(P) support-function gap and a non-corner finite witness."}
seed_list: We introduce the overlap-restricted Choquet--OT envelope for finite-grid DTE CDF paths and conjecture it is the sharp identified set under covariate-specific marginal-overlap envelopes.
We give a finite-support LP/OT diagnostic G_eta(D) with explicit variables, constraints, and dimension that certifies when stacked pointwise DTE bounds are incompatible with one coupling.
We classify dependence restrictions into a testability lattice and conjecture necessary-and-sufficient conditions for marginally identified counterfactual DTEs to become point identified.
We prove or refute sharp pasting for lifted OT relaxations that combine sensitivity envelopes, IV/dose restrictions, and local rank-copula tubes into one full-data DTE law.
We derive uniform weak limits for overlap-constrained lower and upper DTE-bound processes when marginal curves, propensity scores, and overlap sets are estimated.
We characterize discrete, count-valued, and mixed-support DTE bounds by a network-flow coupling representation that does not rely on continuous rearrangement limits.
We define a critical overlap scalar eta_star(P,D) at which the DTE path set first becomes non-rectangular relative to pointwise Makarov bounds.
We characterize when covariate aggregation of conditional DTE envelopes is uniformly sharp and when aggregation strictly loses pathwise coupling information.
We prove an equivalence between the OT support-function envelope and a random-set Choquet capacity for DTE paths under marginal-overlap restrictions.
We construct a finite certificate for falsifying mutually stochastic increasing or rank-copula restrictions from overlap-restricted DTE marginal data.

seed_details.one_line: We introduce the overlap-restricted Choquet--OT envelope for finite-grid DTE CDF paths and conjecture it is the sharp identified set under covariate-specific marginal-overlap envelopes.

anchor_paper.author: Firpo and Ridder
anchor_paper.claim: Makarov bounds are pointwise sharp but not uniformly sharp for finite vectors or functionals of the DTE CDF; covariates can tighten bounds.
anchor_paper.location: abstract and main finite-vector sharpness discussion

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP warning avoided: the focal object is a non-LP support-function/Choquet--OT envelope, with finite LP only as a diagnostic.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from FirpoRidder2019 because their finite-vector correction already handles simultaneous DTE CDF points.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: FirpoRidder2019 fixes marginals, while this seed allows covariate-specific marginal-overlap envelopes and requires measurable conditional coupling pasting.

adversarial_referee.instance: Envelope may lack a named computable focal object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: h_eta_P(v;D) and C_eta(P;D) are named support-function objects with finite-support evaluation.

adversarial_referee.instance: Sharp equality could only prove inclusion from couplings into halfspaces.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patch: proof route explicitly requires the converse via Aumann support-function representation.

adversarial_referee.instance: Strictness may be shown only by a hand-tuned distribution.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patch: Assumption path-conflict gives an open OT-face separation, and Section 9 gives a concrete non-corner witness.

seed_details.one_line: We give a finite-support LP/OT diagnostic G_eta(D) with explicit variables, constraints, and dimension that certifies when stacked pointwise DTE bounds are incompatible with one coupling.

anchor_paper.author: prior proposal reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: Add a computable finite-support LP/OT diagnostic with explicit dimension and constraints and bound width for Pr{Delta <= d}.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends finite LP machinery, but non-trivial because the diagnostic is pathwise and detects incompatible extremal couplings, not just endpoint width.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Russell2021 because sharp bounds with constraints are already computational LPs.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Russell2021 is general-functional computation; the seed contributes a DTE-path incompatibility scalar tied to marginal-overlap cells.

adversarial_referee.instance: LP diagnostic may be field-tier without a new scalar.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: G_eta(D) is a named operational scalar with certificate direction v.

adversarial_referee.instance: Russell2021 may already compute comparable finite support functionals.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: closest gap is pathwise DTE compatibility, not scalar functional endpoints.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only Pr{Delta <= d} may be diagnosed, not the full CDF path.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patch: seed is phrased for finite-grid path D, with pointwise d as a readout.

seed_details.one_line: We classify dependence restrictions into a testability lattice and conjecture necessary-and-sufficient conditions for marginally identified counterfactual DTEs to become point identified.

anchor_paper.author: Frandsen and Lefgren
anchor_paper.claim: Mutually stochastic increasing potential outcomes tighten DTE bounds and imply testable restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens/varies dependence assumptions; non-trivial only if the output is an iff lattice with testability labels, not another sufficient restriction.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from FrandsenLefgren2021 because they already give testable dependence restrictions for DTE bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: they give one positive-dependence class, not a necessary-and-sufficient taxonomy across rank, copula, MTP2, and sensitivity classes.

adversarial_referee.instance: The lattice may list sufficient conditions only.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: flagship version requires necessity and falsifiability labels for each class.

adversarial_referee.instance: May identify only the CDF, not quantiles or harmed fractions.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patch: one primitive copula class should jointly cover CDF, quantile, harmed-share, and benefit-share functionals.

adversarial_referee.instance: Some copula restrictions may already be fully catalogued in probability literature.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Risk lands partly; score novelty lower unless tied to overlap-marginal DTE data.

seed_details.one_line: We prove or refute sharp pasting for lifted OT relaxations that combine sensitivity envelopes, IV/dose restrictions, and local rank-copula tubes into one full-data DTE law.

anchor_paper.author: Tan, Blanchet and Syrgkanis
anchor_paper.claim: PRTE partial identification with limited IV support is formulated as constrained conditional OT and reduced to sharp one-dimensional OT subproblems.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/pasting result is non-trivial only if it targets the named CCOT sharpness mechanism and gives open-class realizability or failure.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026 because separable CCOT already proves sharpness.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: their separability is for PRTE mean functionals; DTE path law with copula tubes requires a single full-data law across restrictions.

adversarial_referee.instance: Refutation may use incompatible artificial cells.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patch: require an open class of observable laws or a constructive pasting theorem.

adversarial_referee.instance: CCOT PRTE is not a DTE CDF comparator.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: comparator is the pasting mechanism, but this is a cross-target bridge; wording must stay precise.

adversarial_referee.instance: No computable evaluation map if pasting is abstract.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patch: lifted OT table and full-data law map must be named outputs.

seed_details.one_line: We derive uniform weak limits for overlap-constrained lower and upper DTE-bound processes when marginal curves, propensity scores, and overlap sets are estimated.

anchor_paper.author: Kaido, Molinari and Stoye
anchor_paper.claim: Calibrated projection confidence intervals provide uniform coverage for functions of partially identified parameter vectors.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends inference machinery; non-trivial if near-binding overlap creates a new nonregular bound-process limit, weaker if routine delta method.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from KaidoMolinariStoye2019 because projection inference already covers functions of identified sets.
seed_details.rebuttal: Probably false but risky: DTE bound functions with estimated support constraints are nonsmooth processes, not fixed finite-dimensional vectors.

adversarial_referee.instance: No new identification object, mostly inference extension.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: lands; downgrade to field/strong field unless a new near-binding overlap limit is isolated.

adversarial_referee.instance: Moment-inequality process inference may already cover it.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Need deeper survey before flagship claim.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only DTE CDF bands, no quantile or functional family.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Could include quantile maps, but not enough for current flagship target.

seed_details.one_line: We characterize discrete, count-valued, and mixed-support DTE bounds by a network-flow coupling representation that does not rely on continuous rearrangement limits.

anchor_paper.author: Mullahy
anchor_paper.claim: Count-valued outcomes create DTE partial-identification issues beyond the continuous case and require appropriate bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends continuous to discrete/mixed support; stronger if mass-point merging changes the extremal-coupling geometry, weaker if just a finite LP.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Mullahy2023 because count-valued DTE bounds are already derived.
seed_details.rebuttal: Partly true for count-only cases; novelty needs mixed support plus overlap restrictions and network-flow sharpness.

adversarial_referee.instance: May only give an algorithm for known finite supports.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patch: include mixed support and exact reduction to Mullahy at integer-only corner.

adversarial_referee.instance: Ordinal/count DTE papers may close special cases.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Risk lands; keep as fallback seed, not chosen.

adversarial_referee.instance: Network-flow representation may be standard LP.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Need a mass-point merging theorem as focal object.

seed_details.one_line: We define a critical overlap scalar eta_star(P,D) at which the DTE path set first becomes non-rectangular relative to pointwise Makarov bounds.

anchor_paper.author: Fan and Park
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp DTE bounds can be tightened with covariates and inference is nonregular for the sharp bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sensitivity critical-delta motif; non-trivial only if eta_star has an independent support-function formula, not defined as the first eta where non-rectangularity holds.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is tautological because eta_star is just the threshold where the desired property changes.
seed_details.rebuttal: The objection lands unless eta_star is defined by an independent OT-face gap; downgrade unless paired with Conjecture 1.

adversarial_referee.instance: Critical eta can be tautological without a closed formula.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: lands; only viable as a corollary of the Choquet--OT envelope.

adversarial_referee.instance: Might only show eta small widens bounds.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs exact sign/nonrectangularity threshold.

adversarial_referee.instance: Threshold may depend on one artificial two-point support.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Require open-class OT-face condition.

seed_details.one_line: We characterize when covariate aggregation of conditional DTE envelopes is uniformly sharp and when aggregation strictly loses pathwise coupling information.

anchor_paper.author: Fan, Guerre and Zhu
anchor_paper.claim: The paper characterizes when covariates shrink identified sets for functionals of the joint distribution of potential outcomes.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends covariate tightening; non-trivial if it gives an iff frontier for aggregation sharpness rather than another covariate-adjusted bound.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from FanGuerreZhu2017 because they already explain when covariates shrink identified sets.
seed_details.rebuttal: False for pathwise DTE coupling: their result is broader functional logic, not a necessary-and-sufficient aggregation frontier for CDF paths.

adversarial_referee.instance: Sufficiency of conditional refinement may be easy; necessity may fail.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Require boundary counterexample showing aggregation loss when condition fails.

adversarial_referee.instance: May handle only CDF path not DTE functionals.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Can state support-function family for all finite grids.

adversarial_referee.instance: FanGuerreZhu2017 may already include DTE-like functionals.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Need section-level verification; keep as second-tier seed.

seed_details.one_line: We prove an equivalence between the OT support-function envelope and a random-set Choquet capacity for DTE paths under marginal-overlap restrictions.

anchor_paper.author: Ober-Reynolds
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp identified sets for moments of the joint potential-outcome distribution are intervals whose endpoints are OT values.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bridge/connection shape; non-trivial if equivalence yields evaluable DTE CDF path capacities, not just renaming OT duality.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from random-set partial identification because capacities already characterize sharp regions.
seed_details.rebuttal: Only partly: the DTE path and marginal-overlap envelopes need a specific capacity with OT support-function evaluation.

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims non-LP representation but may only restate support functions.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Need a Choquet integral formula and explicit OT dual, otherwise downgrade.

adversarial_referee.instance: No comparator chain beyond Ober-Reynolds.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patch by pairing Ober-Reynolds with Beresteanu-Molchanov-Molinari random-set literature.

adversarial_referee.instance: Equivalence may be formal but not sharpness-improving.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs strict pathwise improvement over pointwise Makarov.

seed_details.one_line: We construct a finite certificate for falsifying mutually stochastic increasing or rank-copula restrictions from overlap-restricted DTE marginal data.

anchor_paper.author: Frandsen and Lefgren
anchor_paper.claim: Mutually stochastic increasing potential outcomes have testable implications and tighten DTE bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/testability seed; non-trivial if the certificate targets named restrictions on an open class, weaker if it tests only a special table.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from FrandsenLefgren2021 because they already provide a test of stochastic increasingness.
seed_details.rebuttal: Mostly true for MSI alone; novelty would need joint overlap/rank-copula certificates, so this is fallback.

adversarial_referee.instance: Named paper already has the testable implication.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: lands; downgrade to field unless rank-copula/overlap interaction is new.

adversarial_referee.instance: Certificate may be a finite artificial table.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Need open-class finite certificate.

adversarial_referee.instance: No sharp identified-set theorem, only falsification.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Add sharp bounds after failed test or keep as field-tier.

proposed_from.literature_map: Makarov1981 and Rueschendorf1982 are the probability anchors for fixed-marginal extremal couplings. HeckmanSmithClements1997 motivates DTE objects beyond mean impacts. FanPark2010 gives sharp pointwise DTE bounds and inference from identified potential-outcome marginals, with covariate tightening. FirpoRidder2019 shows pointwise Makarov bounds are not uniformly sharp for finite CDF vectors or functionals. FanGuerreZhu2017 studies functionals of the joint potential-outcome distribution and when covariates shrink identified sets. Russell2021 and OberReynolds2023 provide computational and OT routes for joint-potential-outcome functionals, but not the marginal-overlap DTE path diagnostic. FrandsenLefgren2021 gives testable positive-dependence restrictions that tighten DTE bounds. Mullahy2023 and LuDingDasgupta2018 show discrete/count/ordinal DTE supports need dedicated sharp analysis. Stoye2009 and KaidoMolinariStoye2019 cover partial-ID inference, but not overlap-constrained DTE-bound processes. FanPassShi2025 and TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026 show current OT methods are moving toward constrained conditional OT, leaving the DTE path-coupling geometry open. The live gap is simultaneous sharpness across DTE CDF ordinates when covariate-specific overlap restrictions bind differently and pointwise extremal couplings need not paste into one full-data law.
